pattern: Treat two words as linkable exactly when their group values differ, their lengths match, and they differ in a single character, then find the longest chain of links while keeping the words in their original order.
hints: Think of each word as only being allowed to attach onto an earlier word when the two have different group values, the same length, and differ in exactly one character.
This becomes a longest-chain dynamic program: for every word, look back at every earlier word that could link to it and extend that word's best chain by one.

Track, for each position, the length of the best chain ending there and which earlier position it came from, then follow the back-links from whichever position has the longest chain to rebuild the answer in order.

constraints.note: words are distinct and use only lowercase English letters

edge_inputs.description: one word links to two different later words through two separate valid two-word chains
every consecutive pair meets all three linking conditions, so the whole array chains together
a single word is trivially the whole answer
equal group values block the link even though the words otherwise match closely
different lengths block the link even when the group values differ
a hamming distance greater than one blocks the link even when group values differ and lengths match
three words that are pairwise compatible in sequence form one continuous three-word chain
two separate equally long valid chains exist among the words, and either is an acceptable answer
linking checks stay correct at the maximum word length

stress.description: random distinct words of varying lengths with random group values, up to the maximum array length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    alphabet = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    words = []
    seen = set()
    while len(words) < n:
        length = rng.randint(1, 10)
        w = ''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(length))
        if w not in seen:
            seen.add(w)
            words.append(w)
    groups = [rng.randint(1, n) for _ in range(n)]
    return [words, groups]
